title: Robbing The Banks

jokes.text: I mean, I had to sell half your coin collection just to buy it.
jokes.setup: Will casually mentions selling Carlton's coin collection
jokes.punchline: The nonchalant admission of theft to buy something for himself
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment - Will's casual confession of selling Carlton's stuff shows his street-smart selfishness. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Will, seek help.
jokes.setup: Carlton is forced to apologize to Will
jokes.punchline: He delivers a backhanded apology that's actually an insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - follows directions while still getting in a dig. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: The honeys might get a little nervous if they see me with a midget.
jokes.setup: Will asks Carlton to drop him off blocks away from the beach
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's because he doesn't want to be seen with Carlton due to his height
jokes.explanation: Typical Will insult humor. Gets the job done but relies on height jokes which are somewhat cheap.

jokes.text: Oh, really? Well, I got ten spankies here that said you're going to the beach.
jokes.setup: Carlton refuses to go to the beach
jokes.punchline: Will threatens physical comedy/spanking to change his mind
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy threat that plays on Will/Carlton dynamic. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: They lied.
jokes.setup: Will's money didn't convince Carlton to go to the beach
jokes.punchline: Carlton's deadpan response that the money 'lied'
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and unexpected personification of money. Carlton's deadpan delivery makes it land perfectly.

jokes.text: How thrilling for you!
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions her bags are upstairs
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic response shows his disdain
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey sarcasm. Well-integrated with character but fairly standard butler wit.

jokes.text: I could very easily rip out your heart, eat it, and then have a cappuccino.
jokes.setup: Pregnant Vivian warns Geoffrey about her mood
jokes.punchline: Escalates to graphic violence followed by casual cappuccino
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from threat to casual coffee. The contrast between violence and mundane activity is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Hallmark couldn't have put it any better.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks if she made herself clear after violent threat
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically compares it to a greeting card
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback to Vivian's threat with perfect ironic comparison. Geoffrey's wit shines through.

jokes.text: Me, too, but I still get things done.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she just wants to lie in bed and eat pie
jokes.punchline: Philip admits he wants the same but claims he's more productive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Philip's self-awareness about his own laziness while trying to maintain superiority.

jokes.text: You paid for your own dinner.
jokes.setup: Hilary says 'You're not going to believe this'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey assumes something actually responsible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey line that shows his expectation of Hilary's selfishness. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Now, if anyone asks, I'm 21 and I fought in Desert Storm.
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions being interviewed about 'the real me'
jokes.punchline: Her 'real' identity involves impossible age and military service
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Hilary moment. The combination of vanity and completely fabricated military service is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: You know, Uncle Phil, if we're lucky, she'll come back as Clair Huxtable.
jokes.setup: Vivian leaves in a bad mood
jokes.punchline: Will references another sitcom mom, breaking the fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke referencing The Cosby Show. Bold fourth-wall break that works because of the show's willingness to be self-aware.

jokes.text: Look, a black Howdy Doody.
jokes.setup: Edward appears at the door
jokes.punchline: Will compares him to the puppet character
jokes.explanation: Basic appearance-based humor. Gets a laugh but relies on cheap visual comparison without much craft.

jokes.text: If it's your nose, I think I know where you left it.
jokes.setup: Edward says he almost forgot one thing
jokes.punchline: Will assumes it's his nose (brown-nosing reference)
jokes.explanation: Solid brown-nosing joke that plays on Edward's obsequious behavior. Well-timed and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Ed, do you want me to get you some knee pads?
jokes.setup: Edward repeatedly thanks Philip
jokes.punchline: Will suggests knee pads for excessive groveling
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the brown-nosing theme with implied physical comedy. Sharp and economical.

jokes.text: Dear Mr. Banks, how can I put this? Your wife is crazy, and you're cheap. L quit, adios, Hector.
jokes.setup: The gardener left a note for Phil
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest resignation letter with blunt personal attacks
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from polite opening to harsh truth. Memorable phrasing and good structure.

jokes.text: Even Aladdin only got three wishes.
jokes.setup: Philip lists multiple tasks for Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey compares it to fairy tale wishes
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that perfectly captures Geoffrey's exasperation. Good structure and character voice.

jokes.text: Your pop had a bit of a drinking problem though, didn't he?
jokes.setup: Philip quotes his father's wisdom about giving vs receiving
jokes.punchline: Will undercuts the sentiment by referencing alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion that undercuts Philip's moral high ground. Dark but perfectly timed and character-appropriate for Will.

jokes.text: A gentleman would at least give me dinner first.
jokes.setup: Philip demands Will get out of his clothes and in the car
jokes.punchline: Will responds as if it's a sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo humor that plays on misinterpreting Philip's command. Gets the job done but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey, Jamal, why don't you offer him this Kit Kat--
jokes.setup: Will tries to give his friend food in court
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate behavior that gets cut off by Philip
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's street mentality clashing with courtroom decorum. Character-appropriate but not particularly witty.

jokes.text: Girl, you look so good, I wish I could plant you and grow a whole field of you all.
jokes.setup: Will hits on the court stenographer
jokes.punchline: Elaborate agricultural metaphor for attraction
jokes.explanation: Creative pickup line that's both smooth and ridiculous. Shows Will's charm and inappropriate timing perfectly.

jokes.text: Kong not happy.
jokes.setup: Philip physically intimidates Will at the bench
jokes.punchline: Will compares him to King Kong
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective King Kong reference. Great economy but relies on basic pop culture comparison.

jokes.text: Word? That's kind of fly, man.
jokes.setup: Defendant mentions writing a book
jokes.punchline: Will inappropriately shows interest during court proceedings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment showing his street mentality in formal setting. Cringe humor from inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Yo, Uncle Judge.
jokes.setup: Will interrupts court proceedings
jokes.punchline: Will addresses Phil with casual street greeting mixed with formal title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - mixing casual and formal inappropriately. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Yo, Uncle Judge.
jokes.setup: Will addressing Phil during court proceedings
jokes.punchline: Combining family relationship with judicial title
jokes.explanation: Clever blend of formal and informal address. Economical and shows Will's unique position in the courtroom.

jokes.text: Love you, too, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Philip says 'I really, really hate you'
jokes.punchline: Will responds with fake affection
jokes.explanation: Classic Will deflection with fake sweetness. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I can get 300 spankies for this. $225, tops. I tried selling it yesterday.
jokes.setup: Will brags about his baseball's increasing value
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey deflates Will's optimism with market reality
jokes.explanation: Good reality check humor. Geoffrey's practical knowledge vs Will's optimism.

jokes.text: And could you pick up my dress from the dry cleaner's?
jokes.setup: Family members keep adding tasks for Luther
jokes.punchline: Tasks escalate beyond home repair to personal errands
jokes.explanation: Part of escalating sequence showing family's exploitation of Luther. Hilary's selfishness shines through.

jokes.text: While you're at it, could you type up your thoughts on the industrial revolution? You know, double space it and put my name on it?
jokes.setup: Continuing list of tasks for Luther
jokes.punchline: Ashley asks him to do her homework and commit plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that shows Ashley's corruption. The casualness of academic dishonesty from the 'good' kid is perfectly subversive.

jokes.text: The pen? Please tell me that's slang for the Valley.
jokes.setup: Will mentions Luther spent time 'in the pen'
jokes.punchline: Carlton desperately hopes it means something innocent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton naivety. His sheltered worldview creates genuine hope for an innocent explanation.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I've got to make myself look less attractive.
jokes.setup: Ashley learns Luther is an ex-con
jokes.punchline: Ashley assumes her looks are the danger, not general crime risk
jokes.explanation: Great teenage self-absorption humor. Ashley's priorities are perfectly wrong for the situation.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I've got to make myself look less attractive. I know, I'll put on something of Mom's.
jokes.setup: Hilary panics about being around an ex-con
jokes.punchline: Her solution reveals vanity - wearing mom's clothes to look worse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Hilary moment that shows her narcissism even in crisis. The backhanded insult to her mother is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You know, Hilary, I don't even think it's possible for you to be prettier than you thought.
jokes.setup: Hilary says she's prettier than she thought
jokes.punchline: Will's comment can be read as compliment or insult
jokes.explanation: Masterfully ambiguous line that works as both compliment and insult. Perfect Will smartass moment with great economy.

jokes.text: But, what could you possibly have of any value?
jokes.setup: Carlton worries about leaving Luther alone in Will's room
jokes.punchline: Carlton insults Will by saying he has nothing worth stealing
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton dig at Will's possessions. Sets up the baseball reveal nicely.

jokes.text: Where's my Willie? Carlton, I can't find my Willie.
jokes.setup: Will is looking for his Willie Mays baseball
jokes.punchline: The phrasing creates sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Basic double entendre that relies on name similarity for cheap laugh. Works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Where's my Willie? Carlton, I can't find my Willie.
jokes.setup: Will looking for his Willie Mays baseball
jokes.punchline: Phrasing creates unintentional innuendo
jokes.explanation: Unintentional double entendre from Will's panic. Simple innuendo humor that works for the character's distress.

jokes.text: If it looks like a duck, smells like a duck and sounds like a duck, what is it? Your prom date?
jokes.setup: Carlton uses duck analogy to prove Luther's guilt
jokes.punchline: Will turns it into insult about Carlton's prom date
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of common saying. Will hijacks Carlton's logic for a personal insult. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: I told you, she had a cold.
jokes.setup: Will insults Carlton's prom date by calling her duck-like
jokes.punchline: Carlton defensively explains his date's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - defensive but makes the insult worse. Great character work.

jokes.text: I told you, she had a cold.
jokes.setup: Carlton's defensive response to prom date insult
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing detail about his prom experience
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that reveals Carlton's social awkwardness. His earnest defense makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, Luther, you dropped your wallet. No, that's yours.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to test Luther's honesty with fake dropped wallet
jokes.punchline: Plan backfires immediately when Luther correctly identifies it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton scheme gone wrong. His amateur detective work fails immediately, creating great cringe comedy.

jokes.text: So it is. How honest of you to say so.
jokes.setup: Luther correctly identifying the wallet as Carlton's
jokes.punchline: Carlton's awkward cover-up of his failed test
jokes.explanation: Carlton trying to save face after his honesty test backfires. Character-appropriate awkwardness in social manipulation.

jokes.text: The word is 'idiot,' man. Can't you see he's innocent?
jokes.setup: Carlton asks for 'a word'
jokes.punchline: Will provides the word as an insult about Carlton's detective skills
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that plays on Carlton's request for 'a word.' Good economy and typical Will/Carlton dynamic.

jokes.text: about them calls you been making to 1-900-HORNY.
jokes.setup: Carlton threatens to tell their dad about Luther
jokes.punchline: Will threatens to expose Carlton's phone sex calls
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke. Cheap sexual humor but good blackmail comedy.

jokes.text: Maybe Luther took all our stuff to get it Scotchgarded.
jokes.setup: Will desperately tries to explain the robbery
jokes.punchline: Suggests ridiculously innocent explanation for theft
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd rationalization. Shows Will's desperate denial with creative but ridiculous logic.

jokes.text: My Tevin Campbell posters. If he touched them, I'll hunt him down like a dog.
jokes.setup: Family discovering what was stolen
jokes.punchline: Ashley's violent threat over teen heartthrob posters
jokes.explanation: Ashley's teenage priorities surfacing in crisis. The specific threat over pop star posters shows character values.

jokes.text: I can't breathe.
jokes.setup: Will discovers his video games are stolen
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated physical reaction to the loss
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy reaction. Gets the job done showing Will's priorities but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: If you had let me go to the beach, then none of this would have never happened.
jokes.setup: Philip blames Will for bringing Luther into their home
jokes.punchline: Will deflects blame with twisted logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will logic - completely self-serving but delivered with conviction. Great callback to opening conflict.

jokes.text: Luther's in the house. Yeah, you're right. Dig me with my bad self, huh?
jokes.setup: Will acts tough about confronting Luther
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to fear when Luther appears, then tries to play it off
jokes.explanation: Classic tough guy/coward flip. Will's bravado immediately crumbles, then he tries to save face.

jokes.text: Yeah, if this wall weren't in my way, mister, you'd be plenty sorry.
jokes.setup: Will confronts Luther angrily
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's hiding behind a wall while acting tough
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy setup. Will's false bravado while hiding creates perfect physical comedy moment.

jokes.text: Damn, that was fast. You know, they must have thought we was white folks.
jokes.setup: Police arrive quickly after being called
jokes.punchline: Will jokes about racial bias in police response times
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered as throwaway line. Addresses real issue with perfect timing and Will's street perspective.

jokes.text: The man is obviously a deviant.
jokes.setup: Officer mentions Edward called 1-900-HORNY
jokes.punchline: Philip's pompous moral judgment
jokes.explanation: Philip's judgmental response plays into his character. Good callback to earlier joke about Carlton's calls.

jokes.text: The man is obviously a deviant.
jokes.setup: Edward robbed them partly over phone sex accusations
jokes.punchline: Phil confirms Edward is perverted, validating the accusation
jokes.explanation: Phil's judgmental response. Character-consistent but predictable.

jokes.text: Yeah, don't work for you.
jokes.setup: Philip asks what they've all learned
jokes.punchline: Luther's blunt response about working for the family
jokes.explanation: Perfect Luther response - direct and honest about his experience. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Yeah, but he took Will's baseball. You mean the one in the plastic case? I borrowed it for batting practice.
jokes.setup: Carlton still suspects Luther took the baseball
jokes.punchline: Ashley reveals she took it for batting practice
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that explains the missing baseball mystery. Ashley's innocence makes the misunderstanding funnier.

jokes.text: Somebody wrote all over it. But, don't worry, I cleaned it off for you.
jokes.setup: Ashley borrowed Will's valuable autographed baseball
jokes.punchline: Ashley destroyed its value by removing the autograph
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic destruction. Ashley's helpfulness ruins everything. Perfect sitcom escalation.

jokes.text: Really, didn't look like it. Somebody wrote all over it. But, don't worry, I cleaned it off for you.
jokes.setup: Ashley mentions the baseball didn't look valuable
jokes.punchline: Reveals she destroyed the autograph by cleaning it
jokes.explanation: Devastating punchline that destroys Will's prized possession through innocent ignorance. Perfect escalation and irony.

jokes.text: baseball game where man with stick hit ball and run. Sort of like this.
jokes.setup: Will explains baseball to someone
jokes.punchline: Will runs away like Carlton did earlier
jokes.explanation: Direct callback to Carlton's earlier escape. Physical comedy repetition for ending.

jokes.text: See, baseball game where man with stick hit ball and run. Sort of like this.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain baseball after his breakdown
jokes.punchline: Gives overly simple explanation while presumably demonstrating
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier baseball explanation. Shows Will's mental state after losing his prize. Decent but not exceptional.

tmdb_overview: Philip is quick to judge an ex-con Will urged him to hire as a handyman and temporary assistant after the house is robbed.
